title: Building Game of Darts
content: Darts is a game enjoyed by millions of people around the world, yet many scoring applications focus on only a small selection of game modes. From the beginning, the goal was to create something more flexible and suitable for a wide range of players.

[The Game of Darts](/apps/gameofdarts) was designed to support a variety of popular darts formats. Alongside traditional games such as 301, 501, and 701, support was added for alternative game modes including Cricket, Snooker, Halve It, All 5s, Hare & Hound, Bob's 27, and 1 to 20.

Another important objective was making scorekeeping fast and straightforward. Players should be able to focus on their game rather than navigating complicated menus or entering unnecessary information.

Statistics and performance tracking became a key part of the project. By recording results over time, players can review their progress, identify strengths and weaknesses, and measure improvement across practice sessions and competitive matches.

Developing The Game of Darts also provided an opportunity to build reusable technology that would later support future BarkinMad Studios projects. Many of the development practices, shared components, analytics systems, and lessons learned during this project helped shape later apps and games.

Although BarkinMad Studios would later expand into retro-inspired arcade gaming through the [ZX Series](/zx-series), [The Game of Darts](/apps/gameofdarts) remains an important milestone in the studio's history and represents the beginning of the journey.

Today, the project continues to demonstrate the studio's commitment to building practical, user-friendly software that helps people enjoy their hobbies and interests while providing useful tools for tracking performance and improvement.
